explanations.4: The `local -r` makes a read-only variable, and `${1:-}` means use the first argument if it exists, otherwise use an empty string.
explanations.7: This line checks if the variable is empty (the `-z` test), and if it is, prints the error message to stderr (the `>&2` part) and continues anyway.
explanations.18: The `command -v` finds programs in your PATH, the `|| true` prevents an error if the command isn't found, and the whole thing is in a subshell (the `$()`) so we can check if it returned anything.
explanations.29: The double quotes inside the string need escaping with backslashes so bash doesn't think the string ends early.
explanations.34: The `2> /dev/null` silences any error messages from docker, and we're comparing the output to an empty string to check if the image doesn't exist.
